page: Microsoft Azure D32s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv5 family. It is powered by an x86_64 architecture Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The configuration features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled at two threads per core. It includes 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance type does not include any local storage or bundled GPUs, relying instead on external Premium Storage for disk operations.

Benchmark results position the D32s_v5 in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks, with a few notable strengths. The single-core stress-ng performance is 1899.39, and the multi-core score is 31047.19. It demonstrates strong memory subsystem performance, as evidenced by a PassMark memory score of 2815.92 and a cached small-block read bandwidth of 1679.64 GB/sec. Image processing benchmarks also rank in the strong tier at 13678.0. However, processing large-scale workloads reveals architectural limits; while small 135M parameter LLM text generation is strong at 275.82 tokens/sec, prompt processing for a large 70B model drops to a weak 6.58 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D32s_v5 offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads that do not require high-speed local NVMe storage or hardware acceleration. The absence of local storage reduces baseline costs but requires provisioning external storage. The instance is suited for enterprise applications, static web serving benchmarked at 33.3 GB/sec, database operations with a PassMark database score of 15034.9, software compilation, and moderate-scale machine learning inference on smaller models.
description: Microsoft Azure D32s_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv5 family, featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz and 128.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPUs, it relies on external Premium Storage. Benchmarks show average CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 31047.19 and a PassMark CPU score of 36986.59. It exhibits strong memory performance, with a PassMark memory score of 2815.92 and 1679.64 GB/sec cached read bandwidth. While text generation for small 135M LLM models is strong at 275.82 tokens/sec, performance on large 70B models is weak at 6.58 tokens/sec. This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering balanced compute and strong memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32s_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, offering balanced compute performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and strong memory performance for enterprise workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM
No local storage, requiring external Premium Storage solutions
Strong memory performance and image processing capabilities
Average compute performance across multi-core and database benchmarks
Suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations
